column24: This week I completed GitLab contributor onboarding and set up my public Contribution README as a development journal for the program. I carefully evaluated several candidate issues using the selection checklist and chose issue #589183, which involves adding tab-based filters to the dashboard listing UI. I documented the problem analysis, expected behavior, and likely affected components in my README, and tagged @gitlab-bot help to confirm scope and begin engagement with a Merge Request Coach.
column25:
Next week, I plan to move into Phase II by setting up the local development environment and familiarizing myself with the GitLab frontend codebase. I will reproduce the current dashboard listing behavior to understand how data is rendered and identify where the tab-based filtering should be integrated.

After reproducing the issue, I plan to locate the relevant components, analyze how similar tab patterns are implemented elsewhere in the codebase, and draft a concrete implementation plan before starting development. I will also review testing patterns in related components to ensure my solution aligns with existing standards.

I completed GitLab onboarding, then forked and cloned the spring-2026-gitlab-ai301 project into my workspace.

I selected and claimed issue #591706 (“Refactor sorting option persistence for group and project list”).

I also updated my Contribution README with a Phase I problem summary plus an initial solution approach and testing plan.
column25:
Next week I plan to set up a working GitLab development environment (GDK or Web IDE) and reliably reproduce the sorting preference behavior described in issue #591706.

I will trace where user_preferences.projects_sort is read and written in the codebase, validate how it affects project vs. group lists, and refine my solution approach based on that.

I also aim to draft clear reproduction steps and a more detailed implementation plan in my Contribution README to complete Phase II.
